REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS GRANTED. PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER TO DISCUSS THE OPTIONS AND TIMELINE FOR NOISE ABATEMENT. PARTIES TO PRESENT A JOINT ORDER GRANTING THE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION AT THE HEARING. IF PARTIES CANNOT AGREE ON A FORM OF ORDER, EACH PARTY TO PREPARE A PROSED ORDER INCLUDING THE SPECIFIC METHOD OF ABATEMENT "REQUIRING DEFENDANTS TO CEASE AND DESIST THE ONGOING MECHANICAL BOILER NOISE EMANATING FROM DEFENDANTS' PROPERTIES," AND SPECIFIC TIMELINE FOR ABATEMENT. THE COMPLETION OF ABATEMENT TO NOT EXCEED 30 DAYS. HARD COPIES OF THE ORDERS TO BE PRESENTED AT THE HEARING. ELECTRONIC COPIES TO BE DELIVERED TO DEPARTMENT501@SFT.ORG. PARTIES ARE TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT PERSPECTIVE ORDERS NO LATER THAN APRIL 23, 2026. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: L. calendar.matters:
Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Further Responses To Form Interrogatories And Special Interrogatories, Set One
Off Calendar May-27-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT 24 HOUR FITNESS USA, LLC'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF'S FURTHER RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES AND SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE

Appearances via Zoom:
Emily Brough of Zacks Freedman & Chernev (415.956.8100, emily@zfcpc.com) for plaintiff Adrienne Leifer;
Katherine Kao of Fennemore LLP (510.834.6600, kkao@fennemorelaw.com) for defendant 24 Hour Fitness USA, LLC;
Jasmine Chappell of Wood Smith Henning & Berman LLP (925.356.8247, jchappell@wshblaw.com) for defendant Seligman Western Enterprises Ltd.

Argued and the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: 24 Hour Fitness USA, LLC's motion to compel further responses to Form Interrogatories Nos. 7.1, 9.1, and 9.2, as well as Special Interrogatories Nos. 30, 31, and 32, is GRANTED.

With respect to Form Interrogatories Nos. 7.1, 9.1, and 9.2, Plaintiff Leifer's responses are incomplete and fail to meaningfully address the substance of the interrogatories at issue. To the extent Plaintiff asserts objections based on attorney-client privilege or the work product doctrine, Plaintiff has failed to adequately identify or support the basis for those objections. Defendants are entitled to discovery about what categories of damages Leifer claims; Leifer has placed them in issue by bringing suit. Although expert discovery has apparently not yet begun, the fact that experts may offer opinions about damages does not shield factual information currently in Leifer's possession from discovery. Leifer shall amend. Her answer must apprise defendants of the nature of the damages she is seeking and any factual information currently possessed by Leifer or her agents. She need not reveal attorney client communications. She may not withhold factual information on the basis that it will also be subject to expert discovery.

Similarly, the responses to Special Interrogatories Nos. 30, 31, and 32 are deficient, incomplete, and nonresponsive. Leifer shall amend.

Code of Civil Procedure, sec. 2030.300, subd. (d) provides that "the court shall impose a monetary sanction" against any party, person, or attorney who unsuccessfully makes or opposes a motion to compel further responses to interrogatories, unless the court finds that the sanctioned party acted with substantial justification or that other circumstances would make the imposition of sanctions unjust. The Court finds that Leifer did not act with substantial justification in opposing the motion. In particular, Leifer offers no authority for the proposition that there is an "expert witness privilege" that allows a party to withhold facts that may later form the basis for expert opinion. The court orders Leifer to pay a reasonable sanction of $2,000 to Defendant 24 Hour Fitness USA, LLC within 30 days.

Proposed order submitted for review. Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Apr-13-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Emily Brough; 415-956-8100; Zacks & Freedman, PC; counsel appearing for plaintiff.
Katherine Kao; 510-834-660; Fennemore LLP; counsel for defendant 24 Hour Fitness appearing via Zoom.
Mark D'Argenio; 925-222-3400; Wood, Smith, Henning & Berman, LLP; counsel for defendant Seligman appearing via Zoom.
Reporter: Miles Lendmay; CSR# 13684; 925-640-4915; mileslendway@gmail

Hearing held. The court informs the parties the court is not considering or ruling on any supplemental filings. The court makes rulings on the objections to 1)declaration of Blake Wells 2) Leifer declaration and 3)declaration of Ms. Brough Argued. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court adopts it's tentative ruling from April 7, 2026 as the matter was continued to rule on the objections. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ADRIENNE LEIFER , AS TRUSTEE OF THE ADRIENNE CARLA LEIFER REVOCABLE TRUST Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction is GRANTED. Parties are ordered to meet and confer to discuss the options and timeline for noise abatement. Parties to present a joint order granting the preliminary injunction at the hearing. If parties cannot agree on a form of order, each party to prepare a prosed order including the specific method of abatement "requiring Defendants to cease and desist the ongoing mechanical boiler noise emanating from Defendants' properties," and specific timeline for abatement. The completion of abatement to not exceed 30 days. Hard copies of the orders to be presented at the hearing. Electronic copies to be delivered to Department501@sft.org.
Parties are to prepare and submit perspective orders no later than April 23, 2026.

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: L. Young; Reported: Miles Lendmay; CSR# 13684; 925-640-4915; mileslendway@gmail (D501)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Apr-07-2026 Continued To Apr-13-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Emily Brough; 415-956-8100; Zacks & Freedman, PC; counsel for plaintiff appearing via Zoom.
Katherine Kao; 510-834-660; Fennemore LLP; counsel for defendant 24 Hour Fitness appearing via Zoom.
Mark D'Argenio; 925-222-3400; Wood, Smith, Henning & Berman, LLP; counsel for defendant Seligman appearing via Zoom.
Reporter: Miles Lendmay; CSR# 13684; 925-640-4915; mileslendway@gmail

Parties inform the court that they could not agree on a form of order. Ms. Brough presents a proposed order. Ms. Kao & Mr. D'Argenio did have a proposed order to present and contests the courts tentative ruling. Ms. Kao states there are new facts and would like the court to rule on evidentiary objections. The court is not aware and suggests a possible continuance in order to review. The parties agree to a continuance of April-13-2026. The court rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF ADRIENNE LEIFER , AS TRUSTEE OF THE ADRIENNE CARLA LEIFER REVOCABLE TRUST Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction is CONTINUED to April-13-2026. All parties waive notice.

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: L. Young; Reported: Reporter: Miles Lendmay; CSR# 13684; 925-640-4915; mileslendway@gmail (D501)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Apr-01-2026 Continued To Apr-07-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Emily Brough; 415-956-8100; Zacks & Freedman; counsel appearing for plaintiff.
Mark D'Argenio; 925-222-3400; Wood, Smith, Henning & Berman, LLP; counsel for defendant Seligman Western Enterprises, LTD. appearing via Zoom.
Katherine Kao; 510-834-6600; Fennemore LLP; counsel for defendant 24 Hour Fitness appearing via Zoom.
Reporter: Jeanine K. Miro; CSR# 11101; 415-320-4664; jmirocsr@gmail.com

Both counsels for defendants contest the courts tentative ruling. Ms.Kao requests clarification in which filings were stricken. Mr. D'Argenio informs the court that he does not debate that opposition was late and supplemental declaration was not authorized. He apologizes to the court and requests the court to still consider filings. Ms. Brough requests the court to adopt the tentative. The court grants the request for reconsideration and requests counsels to agree on a date to continue the matter so the court may review the filings. The court rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF ADRIENNE LEIFER , AS TRUSTEE OF THE ADRIENNE CARLA LEIFER REVOCABLE TRUST Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction is CONTINUED to April 7, 2026. All parties waive notice.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One
Off Calendar Feb-02-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF ADRIENNE LEIFER's, AS TRUSTEE OF THE ADRIENNE CARLA LEIFER REVOCABLE TRUST, Motion To Compel Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff Adrienne Leifer's motion to compel further responses to Requests for Admission, Set One is DENIED as moot. Defendant Seligman Western Enterprises, Ltd. served amended responses on January 21, 2026. Plaintiff does not challenge the mootness of the motion but still seeks sanctions. Under Code of Civil Procedure section 2033.290(d), "[t] he court shall impose a monetary sanction under Chapter 7 (commencing with Section 2023.010) against any party, person, or attorney who unsuccessfully makes or opposes a motion to compel further response, unless it finds that the one subject to the sanction acted with substantial justification or that other circumstances make the imposition of the sanction unjust." The court finds that Defendant did not act with substantial justification in opposing the present motion. Defendant is ordered to pay $1,060 in sanctions to Plaintiff, payable within 10 court days of entry of this order.

Judge: Christopher C. Hite. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/CCH)
